description: A series of protests in Iceland following the 2008 financial crisis where citizens used pots and pans to demand the resignation of the government. The movement led to the first successful overthrow of a government via public pressure in post-war Iceland.

key_events.description: First major 'Kitchenware Revolution' protest occurs outside the Althing.
Demonstrations peak in Reykjavik with thousands demanding the Prime Minister's resignation.
The coalition government of Geir Haarde resigns.
New elections scheduled and constitutional reform process begins.

calibration_notes: A rare instance of near-unanimous public sentiment shift against an incumbent political entity.
